texts.orig: Ion exchange membranes are essential for electrodialysis. However, the presence of multivalent ions, such as Ca 2 þ , Mg 2 þ , or CO3 2 � , SO4 2 � , may result in a detrimental risk of membrane scaling. Mono-valent cation selective membranes may solve this problem. In this study, the surfactant N,N-dimethyl-N-2propenyl-2-propene-1-ammonium chloride-2-propenamide (poly-quaternium-7, PQ7) is used to modi fi cate the cation exchange membranes. Different concentrations of degraded polyquaternium-7 and sodium hydroxide are investigated to determine the optimal point. The composition and characteristics of the membranes were determined with Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and Atomic Force Microscopy (AFM). Hydrophilic and functional groups of the membrane were determined by the water uptake, contact angle and ion exchange capacity. Current -voltage curves were measured to characterize the transport properties of membranes. The obtained results showed that the limiting current was reduced while the Ohmic and electro-convection resistances were increased. Diffusion dialysis experiments have demonstrated that leakage of modi fi ed membrane is lower. Furthermore, a series of electrodialysis experiments was conducted to evaluate the monovalent selectivity of the unmodifi ed and modifi ed CEMs. The leakage of Zn 2 þ was decreased from 22.0% to 14.2% and the leakage of Ca 2 þ and Mg 2 þ were both decreased while the membranes are used in seawater concentration. The obtained results indicate that the membrane has an excellent performance, particularly with respect to selectivity.

texts.orig: However, except mono-valent sodium (Na þ ) and chloride (Cl � ) ions, a signi fi cant amount of multivalent ions such as calcium (Ca 2 þ ), magnesium (Mg 2 þ ) and sulfate (SO4 2 � ) are present in the concentrated seawater. The concentration of ions is two times higher than the original seawater due to the desalination process [7]. Therefore, undesirable precipitation caused by Ca 2 þ , Mg 2 þ and SO4 2 � might happen. This tends to result in membrane scaling, which in turn hinder the membrane performance, for instance, decreasing the current ef fi ciency and increasing the energy consumption [8,9]. Furthermore, in order to produce edible salts with a high purity, multivalent ions are undesired and should preferably be removed.

However, electrodialysis with a selective permeability between monovalent and divalent ions may be a more ef fi cient approach to treat the scaling from RO concentrate. With mono-valent ion selective membranes, mono-valent cations and anions can be separated from multivalent ions. This technology would further expand the applications of electrodialysis. texts.orig: Some methods have been proposed to achieve this goal by surface modi fi cation of membranes. It is mainly based on the immersion of the membrane in a cationic polyelectrolyte solution, electro-deposition, photo-induced polymerization of some conducting polymers [11,12], and annealing treatment as well [13]. However, most materials used for the membrane modi fi cation, including polyethyleneimine [14,15], polyaniline [1 1], and polypyrrole [16], are generally hazardous and/or costly. Furthermore, the modi fi ed layer has a risk of the detachment between the modi fi ed layer and the base membrane due to a weak interaction. Therefore, effective materials for the purpose of a better interaction and lower hazard are demanded. Polyquaternium-7 may be a preferable candidate. It is a polymeric quaternary ammonium salt, consisting of acrylamide and dimethyl diallyl ammonium chloride monomers. The presence of amide groups promote the Hofmann degradation reactions on the chain. The resulted amine group through Hofmann's reaction can then react with a sulfonyl chloride group on the membrane surface. This limits the detachment of the modi fi ed layer from the base membrane due the fact that a cationic charged layer on the base membrane is formed by the sulfonamide bonding.

texts.orig: In the current work, the modifi ed polyquaternium-7 by Hofmann's reaction was used to form a sulfonamide bond at the membrane surface for alleviating the detachment of the modi fi ed layer. A series of electrodialysis experiments with respect to H þ / Zn 2 þ system were conducted to evaluate the mono-valent selectivity of the unmodi fi ed and modi fi ed CEMs using a simulated concentrated seawater. Concentrated sea water contained Ca 2 þ and Mg 2 þ was used to identify the performance of the membranes.

texts.orig: In this step, a Hofmann degradation reaction was induced to increase the reaction activity of polyquaternium-7, because the existence of carbonyl group has a negative effect on the activity of N -H group from the polyquaternium-7 [17]. The protocol for this reaction is as follows: fi rstly, 100 mL of 20% NaOH was dissolved in a 250 mL three-necked fl ask and 30 mL bromine was added drop by drop. After that, 10 g polyquaternium-7 was added in the threenecked fl ask at � 15 1 C and kept mixing for an hour. Then the temperature was increased to 0 1 C for another 1 h. Finally, the mixture solution after reaction was precipitated by methanol after being stirred at 45 1 C for 4 h. The precipitate was dissolved in 0.1 M HCl to remove carbonyl after fi ltration. When no bubbles were formed, the solution was treated using the method of dissolve -precipitate to remove the impurities. The desired product was dried in a vacuum drying chamber. The reaction mechanism is shown in Fig. 1.

texts.orig: 2.3. Preparation of mono-valent cation selective membranes
texts.text: 2.3. Preparation of mono-valent cation selective membranes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Before the experiment, the cationic exchange membrane was rinsed with deionized water in an ultrasonic bath for 15 min to remove excess sodium chloride. The membrane surface was dried with a tissue, and immersed in toluene for 24 h to fully exchange water. After that, the membrane was kept in fresh toluene with a 250 ml three-necked fl ask. Thionylchloride was titrated into the solution at 30 1 C under re fl ux condition. The temperature was increased by the step of 10 1 C per hour until it reached 80 1 C and then kept for 24 h. Following that, the membrane was rinsed with fresh toluene for several times. The modi fi cation was carried out by amination; the chlorosulfonation membrane reacted with degraded polyquaternium-7 in sodium carbonate solution to form a thin layer on the membrane surface. Sodium hydroxide was used as the deacidi fi cation reagent. Sodium hydroxide was selected because of the fact that amines have the ability of nucleophilic reactions; a deacidi fi cation reagent should be alkali, with low nucleophilicity, and is used to absorb the acids that the reaction generates. In addition, the deacidi fi cation reagent itself is a catalyst and is stable enough to have no negative impact on the reaction. The reaction procedure is shown in Fig. 2. The formed sulfonamide bond between amine groups and the surface layer is postulated to prolong the lifetime of the mono-valent cation selective membrane.

texts.orig: The most generally accepted de fi nition of IEC is that it corresponds to the number of counter-ion equivalents in a speci fi ed quantity of a material [18]. A comparison of unmodifi ed and modifi ed membranes was made on this basis. The IEC determination was performed using titration. The membranes were kept in 1 M HCl solution for 24 h and then washed with deionized water to remove the traces of HCl. texts.text: The most generally accepted de fi nition of IEC is that it corresponds to the number of counter-ion equivalents in a speci fi ed quantity of a material [18]. A comparison of unmodifi ed and modifi ed membranes was made on this basis. The IEC determination was performed using titration. The membranes were kept in 1 M HCl solution for 24 h and then washed with deionized water to remove the traces of HCl. Thereafter, the cleaned membranes were immersed in 50 ml of 0.5 M NaCl solution for 24 h. The liberated H þ ions in solution were then titrated with 0.01 M NaOH using phenolphthalein as indicator. The IEC can be calculated from the following equation:

texts.orig: IEC ¼ nH þ md ð 2 Þ

texts.orig: The AFM images also con fi rm that after the chemical modi fi cation, the membrane has a smoother surface. The roughness parameter of the AFM image reveals that the unmodi fi ed membrane has a higher surface roughness ( Ra ¼ 0.216 m m) than modi fi ed membrane ( Ra ¼ 0.172 m m). The high surface roughness of the unmodifi ed membrane is mainly related to the uneven distribution of the fi bers of the non-woven reinforcement, as can be seen from Fig. 5a. The decrease of Rmax from 2.46 m m to 1.85 m m might be the adhesion of the coating layer. A high roughness results in a larger contact area between coating material and membrane surface, so the coating might preferentially accumulate in the valleys of rougher surfaces, and smooth the surface [12]. As a result, the mean-square error reduced from 0.296 m mto 0.225 m m after the chemical modi fi cation.

texts.text: 3.3. Contact angle measurements and water uptake

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Contact angle measurements show that changes take place at the very outer layer of materials during their surface treatment [28]. In general, the membranes which comprise the same chemical composition tend to absorb the same amount of water if the density of ionizable groups is the same throughout the membrane [29,30]. After this chemical modi fi cation, the contact angle of the modi fi ed membrane decreased signi fi cantly, from  120 1 to around  97 1 . This is caused by the degraded polyquaternium-7 on the membrane surface. Incorporation of degraded polyquaternium-7 in the membrane matrix results in an increase of the hydrophilicity. Thus, this modi fi cation may increase the water uptake of the membrane.

texts.orig: The current -voltage curve was investigated to study the interfacial phenomena of the modi fi ed membrane. This typical current -voltage curve can provide valuable information about the transport of ions in and near the ion exchange membrane. At a low current density in the Ohmic region, Ohmic resistances ( Rohm ) are calculated from the slope of the curve in the Ohmic region. Here, the system resistance is attributed by the ionic transport. The next region is the current-limiting region. In this region the current density varies slightly with the increase of the potential. The limiting current ( Rlim ) is estimated by taking the current values at the in fl ection point of the curve in the limiting current region. When the slope of the current -potential curve increases drastically again, the electro-convective region is reached. The currents in this region are larger than the limiting current. Electro-convective resistances ( Rec ) are calculated from the slope of the curve in this region.

texts.orig: As it is shown in Fig. 9, 0.25 mol/L HCl was applied to measure the Current -voltage curves. It can be seen that the limiting current measured using an unmodi fi ed membrane was higher than using a modifi ed membrane. This is due to the fact that the formation of the modi fi ed layer enhances concentration polarization. Furthermore, the region of limiting current expanded. A longer plateau length may arise from the following reasons. Firstly, the multivalent ion cannot permeate the modi fi ed membrane. This results in the great expansion of the area of the retention layer. Only when a higher current is applied can the retention layer be damaged. Secondly, the surface hydrophobicity is an important parameter that affects the development of electro-convection. As the membrane becomes more hydrophilic, the limiting current density and the overlimiting current density at the same potential difference diminished [34]. This behavior corresponds to an increase of the plateau length [12]. Thirdly, surface roughness, which can determine the distribution of the conducting sites of the membrane, causes a diversi fi cation of plateau length. Since the surface roughness decreased, the plateau length increased [35]. The Ohmic resistance increased somewhat. This is due to the fact that the thin modi fi ed layer does not have a signi fi cant in fl uence when the current density is small. The increase of electroconvective resistances is ascribed to the fact that the modi fi ed layer contributes to hinder the transport of ions through the membrane at a high current density.

texts.orig: In Fig. 11, the zero value represents the unmodi fi ed membrane, with the ion leakage of 22%. With the increase of the concentration of sodium hydroxide, the leakage of Zn 2 þ had a signi fi cant decrease before the NaOH concentration reached 5%, and then it levels off. When the NaOH concentration reached 10%, Zn 2 þ ion has the minimum leakage rate. However, when the concentration of sodium hydroxide continued to increase, the leakage of Zn 2 þ ions had slightly increased due to the partial hydrolyzation of the sulfonamide bond, which decreased the thickness of the modi fi ed layer. Therefore, the optimum content of NaOH was determined to be 10%.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this study, a commercial membrane (CSO) was used as a reference to determine the difference with the modi fi ed membrane. The obtained results reveal that the membrane modifi ed by polyquaternium-7 has an obvious decrease in Zn 2 þ leakage, compared to the unmodi fi ed membrane (leakage of Zn 2 þ decreased from 22.0% to 14.2%). However, the leakage is still higher than that in the commercial membrane (leakage of Zn 2 þ is 9.1%). This is due to a number of surface sulfonic acid chloride converted back to sulfonate because of hydration, which in turn reduces the membrane selectivity. Furthermore, during the process, the deacidi fi cation reagent was added to accelerate the reaction and minimized the sulfonic acid chloride reaction with water.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As shown in Table 2, the leakage of Ca 2 þ and Mg 2 þ ions decreased. The similar results of the Zn 2 þ leakage proved that the polyquaternium-7 can be used for surface modi fi cation of CEMs to improve their selectivity. It is worth to note that the selective separation between Ca 2 þ and Mg 2 þ ions by modi fi ed CEMs should be expected to be the same, if only from the aspect of electrostatic exclusion. However, the measured results indicate a different case. This may ascribe to the fact that the size of ions may also affect the transport of ions. The larger the ion, the more its transport through the membrane is sterically hindered. The hydrated Mg 2 þ ion has a radius of 0.429 nm [36], whereas the hydrated Ca 2 þ ion has a radius of 0.412 nm [37]. The similar results of the Zn 2 þ leakage proved that the polyquaternium-7 can be used for surface modi fi cation of CEMs to improve their selectivity. It is worth to note that the selective separation between Ca 2 þ and Mg 2 þ ions by modi fi ed CEMs should be expected to be the same, if only from the aspect of electrostatic exclusion. texts.orig: The membrane modifi ed by polyquaternium-7 was proved to be potentially effective. The results clearly demonstrate that the transport of divalent cations was affected by the presence of the modifi ed layer. The degraded polyquaternium-7 of the modi fi ed membrane was optimized at 3% while the content of NaOH was 10%. Current -voltage curves were used to assess ion transport through the membrane. The limiting current decreased and the current-limiting region expanded due to the formation of the new layer. Compared to the commercial membrane, the modifi ed membrane has a smaller resistance at lower potential. When the modifi ed membrane was used at the seawater concentration, it performed similarly to the commercial reference membrane with the respect to Ca 2 þ and Mg 2 þ leakage. This study demonstrates the potential for further research on mono-valent selective membranes based on chemical bonds.
